question.text: Commodity Index Options as permitted by SEBI are which style of options?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Commodity index options are European-style (exercisable only at expiry) and cash-settled. All ITM contracts are auto-exercised unless buyer gives contrary instruction. **The Trap:** They are not American-style (exercisable any time). Physical settlement is not applicable for index derivatives (no physical commodity to deliver for an index). **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII covers European vs American options in equity derivatives. Series I covers currency option settlement styles. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** European-style means no early exercise risk for option sellers — simplifying risk management compared to American-style options.
